sections.paragraph:
ποταμοφόρητος, ον (φορητός ‘borne, carried’ [φέρω]; PAmh 85, 16 [78 a.d.]; PTebt 610; PStras 5, 10; PFlor 368, 12 al. in pap) pert. to being borne along involuntarily by a flow of water, swept away by a river, overwhelmed by a stream ἵνα αὐτὴν π. ποιήσῃ that he might sweep her away with the stream i.e. drown her Rv 12:15.—AWikenhauser, BZ 6, 1908, 171; 7, 1909, 48. —DELG s.v. φέρω. M-M. TW.

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PStras PStras = Griechische Papyrus der kaiserlichen Universitäts- und Landesbibliothek zu Strassburg—List 4

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

BZ BZ = Biblische Zeitschrift—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 856
